chapter_context.summary: A call to test the spirits to see if they are from God. John provides the ultimate definition of God's character: 'God is love,' and explains how this love is manifested in us.

outline.explanation: This section focuses on Test the Spirits (4:1-6), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 John.

outline.text: God is Love and the Source of our Love
outline.explanation: This section focuses on God is Love and the Source of our Love (4:7-21), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 John.

1.commentary:
### Tyndale Study Notes
**Chapter Overview: 1 John 4**
This verse contributes to the central message of 1 John, emphasizing the grace and truth revealed in Jesus Christ.
1.full_commentary:
### Matthew Henry Commentary
Matthew Henry offers devotional reflections on the spiritual impact of this passage for the believer.

### Jamieson-Fausset-Brown
JFB analysis explores the historical context and original language nuances of this portion of the New Testament.

devotional.question: How does this passage impact your daily walk with God?

cultural_historical.title: New Testament Context
cultural_historical.text: Written in the 1st century AD to diverse groups of believers across the Roman Empire.
